Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Cash-strapped SA Women’s Hockey Team raises funds to get to Olympics

The South African Women’s Hockey Team has had to overcome major financial hurdles to get to the Paris Olympics.

With just four days to go until Paris 2024,SouthAfrica’s women’s hockeyteam has had to defy financial odds to fulfill their Olympic dream.

In South Africa, resources are limited for non-professional sports like hockey and the women’s team has had to resort tocrowdfundingfor assistance.

Although they haven’t yet met their funding goal, the players have persevered with their preparations. With the available funds the team recently embarked on an Asian exposure tour, which culminated in India.

However, head coach Giles Bonnet has rued missed opportunities for further match practice due to the lack of finances.

“Funding has been our biggest challenge, we have had to withdraw from three events, which means about 30 test matches of experience have been lost to this group,” Bonnet toldThe Times of India.

“On top of that we have not been able to train in a high-performance environment as frequently as we would have liked.”

Ranked 18th in the world, the South African women’s team faces a formidable challenge in Group B, which includes hockey powerhouses like Australia, Argentina, Great Britain, Spain, and the United States.

Nonetheless, Bonnet has set his team a realistic target of finishing between 5th – 8th place.

“Just to put things into perspective, the USA received $1 million dollars for their program to Paris,” Bonnet toldgsport.co.zain April.

“This is equal to almost R19 million rand. We could run a fully professional program for four years on this value.”

The SA Women’s Hockey Team has had the good fortune of receiving financial support from Marc Jury at SuperSport over the past two years, while Supersport Schools and Kelvin Watt recently helped the squad travel to China and India.

“There have also been hockey people whom have given up their time to source funding for the team and for this we are very grateful, it has kept our program going,” Bonnet acknowledged.

As the only African team travelling to the Olympics, theSouth African women’s hockey sidecontinues to dominate in Africa.

In 10 Africa Cup of Nations and African Games tournaments since 1994, the women’s team has taken gold in all of them.

But it’s on the global stage where the side has struggled.

“It’s impossible to compete with other countries, the small details will always be missing,” Bonnet added. “The talent exists in our country but once they finish school the gap widens into a chasm that’s not possible to cross anymore.”

“The strong tertiaries are not the high performance solution for international hockey. Unfortunately, this is the reality of our sport currently, but it’s not the end game.”

The SA Women’s Hockey Team will hit off their Paris Olympics campaign against Australia on Sunday (12:45).

title: Cash-strapped SA Women's Hockey Team raises funds to get to Olympics
author: Nick Pawson
description: The South African Women's Hockey Team has had to overcome major financial hurdles to get to the Paris Olympics.
